# **Computational Architecture and Algorithmic Specifications of Vedic Astrological Shadbala and Bhava Bala Engines**

The computational modeling of Vedic astrology requires the transcription of ancient spherical astronomy and horoscopic principles into deterministic algorithms. The foundational metric for evaluating the potency, efficacy, and quantitative strength of planetary bodies (Grahas) and astrological houses (Bhavas) is the Shadbala (Six-fold Strength) and Bhava Bala frameworks. The architectural implementation of these calculations mandates exact celestial coordinates, highly precise ephemeris data, and rigorous programmatic logic to resolve piecewise functions, modulo arithmetic, and complex spherical geometry.

The primary scalar unit utilized across all planetary and house strength modules is the Virupa, also referred to in classical literature as the Shashtiamsa. The system operates on a sexagesimal base, mirroring the timekeeping and geometric frameworks of antiquity. The conversion scalar between the base unit and the macroscopic unit is strictly defined as sixty Virupas equating to one Rupa.1 Engine architectures must output floating-point values in Virupas at each intermediate step, which are then aggregated and subsequently evaluated against defined absolute Rupa thresholds to determine the binary functional state of a planet (strong versus weak).1

The total Shadbala output is the arithmetic sum of six distinct force vectors: Sthana Bala (Positional), Dig Bala (Directional), Kala Bala (Temporal), Cheshta Bala (Motional), Naisargika Bala (Natural), and Drig Bala (Aspectual).1 Furthermore, the computational engine evaluates the 12 houses to generate the Bhava Bala, utilizing the planetary outputs as foundational variables.5 The standard computational model excludes the lunar nodes (Rahu and Ketu) from the primary Shadbala matrix, as they are non-luminous mathematical intersections lacking physical mass, declination variance, or observable kinematics required for these specific algorithms.2

## **STHANA BALA: Positional Strength Matrices**

Sthana Bala evaluates a planet's quantitative strength based solely on its zodiacal placement and dimensional dignity, isolating spatial positioning from temporal anomalies or aspectual interference. The total Sthana Bala is the mathematical summation of five independent sub-components, yielding a theoretical maximum strength of approximately 480 Virupas (8 Rupas) depending on the configuration.9

### **1\. Uchcha Bala (Exaltation Strength)**

Uchcha Bala quantifies the angular distance of a planet from its point of deepest debilitation (Neecha). The algorithm operates on the astronomical principle that a planet at its exact degree of exaltation possesses maximum energetic amplitude, which diminishes linearly as it approaches its exact degree of debilitation. The function maps a maximum spherical distance of ![][image1] to a peak value of 60 Virupas.4

**Input Variables:** The engine requires ![][image2], representing the continuous sidereal longitude of the planet in decimal degrees (![][image3] to ![][image4]). It also requires ![][image5], the exact sidereal longitude of the planet's debilitation point.10

| Planet | Exaltation Point | Debilitation Point (Ld​) | Decimal Degrees (Ld​) |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Sun | Aries ![][image6] | Libra ![][image6] | ![][image7] |
| Moon | Taurus ![][image8] | Scorpio ![][image8] | ![][image9] |
| Mars | Capricorn ![][image10] | Cancer ![][image10] | ![][image11] |
| Mercury | Virgo ![][image12] | Pisces ![][image12] | ![][image13] |
| Jupiter | Cancer ![][image14] | Capricorn ![][image14] | ![][image15] |
| Venus | Pisces ![][image16] | Virgo ![][image16] | ![][image17] |
| Saturn | Libra ![][image18] | Aries ![][image18] | ![][image19] |

**Mathematical Formula:** The algorithm calculates the shortest spherical arc distance ![][image20] between the true position of the planet and its debilitation constant.10

![][image21]  
The resulting distance ![][image20] (which strictly falls within the bounds of ![][image22]) is then scaled by a factor of 3 to yield the Virupa output.

![][image23]  
**Output Parameter:** Float value ranging from ![][image24] to ![][image25] Virupas.10

### **2\. Saptavargaja Bala (Seven-Divisional Dignity Strength)**

This complex algorithmic component evaluates the harmonic dignity of a planet across seven specific divisional charts (Saptavarga): D-1 (Rasi), D-2 (Hora), D-3 (Drekkana), D-7 (Saptamsha), D-9 (Navamsha), D-12 (Dwadashamsha), and D-30 (Trimshamsha).10

**Algorithmic Logic and Dignity Matrices:** For each of the seven vargas, the engine must determine the planet's localized dignity in the sign it occupies. This dignity is derived from a compound relationship matrix known as Panchadha Maitri (Five-fold Friendship), which synthesizes Naisargika (Natural, fixed friendship) and Tatkalika (Temporary, proximity-based friendship).4

Once the combined dignity state per varga is calculated, the engine assigns an exact discrete Virupa scalar. Exaltation and debilitation are explicitly bypassed in this specific subroutine, and Moolatrikona is evaluated strictly by sign placement rather than specific degree constraints.10

| Dignity Classification | Associated State | Virupa Scalar |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Moolatrikona | Root Trine | 45.0 |
| Swakshetra | Own Sign | 30.0 |
| Adhi Mitra | Extreme Friend | 20.0 |
| Mitra | Friend | 15.0 |
| Sama | Neutral | 10.0 |
| Satru | Enemy | 4.0 |
| Adhi Satru | Extreme Enemy | 2.0 |

**Mathematical Formula:** The Saptavargaja Bala is the unweighted scalar sum of the dignity values across all seven charts.10

![][image26]  
**Output Parameter:** Integer value ranging from ![][image27] (Extreme Enemy in all seven vargas) to ![][image28] (Moolatrikona in all seven vargas) Virupas.10

### **3\. Ojayugmarasyamsa Bala (Odd/Even Parity Strength)**

This subroutine computes parity resonance. The algorithm tests whether a planet's sign placement aligns with its inherent energetic gender. It evaluates this independently in both the macro-zodiac D-1 (Rasi) chart and the micro-zodiac D-9 (Navamsha) chart.10

**Input Variables:**

* **![][image29]**: Sign index in D-1 (Aries \= 1, Taurus \= 2,..., Pisces \= 12).  
* ![][image30]: Sign index in D-9.  
* ![][image31]: The defined gender polarity of the planet.  
  * Female: Moon, Venus  
  * Male: Sun, Mars, Jupiter  
  * Neutral: Mercury, Saturn

**Mathematical Formula:**

Let the parity function ![][image32]. If ![][image33], the sign is geometrically Odd; if ![][image34], the sign is geometrically Even.

For each chart ![][image35]:

* If ![][image36] and ![][image37]: Assign 15 Virupas.  
* If ![][image38] and ![][image39]: Assign 15 Virupas.  
* Otherwise: Assign 0 Virupas.

The total score is the sum of the D-1 evaluation and the D-9 evaluation. **Output Parameter:** Integer value strictly restricted to ![][image40] Virupas.10

### **4\. Kendradi Bala (Angular Potency)**

Kendradi Bala algorithmically scales the active power of a planet based on the geometric quadrant of the astrological house (Bhava) it occupies. The engine must query the house index ![][image41] for each planet.4

**Mathematical Formula:** Let $H\_p \\in $ represent the house index. The function applies a discrete scaling factor corresponding to the angular momentum of the house classification.10

![][image42]  
**Output Parameter:** Integer value strictly restricted to ![][image43] Virupas.10

### **5\. Drekkana Bala (Decanate Placement Strength)**

This metric introduces micro-zodiacal precision by evaluating the planet's fractional degree placement within its occupied sign, identifying the specific ![][image6] decanate (Drekkana) it falls into, and matching it against planetary gender polarities.10

**Input Variables:**

* **![][image44]**: The continuous fractional degree of the planet normalized to its current sign $  
* If ![][image36] and ![][image45]: Assign 15 Virupas.10  
* If ![][image46] and ![][image47]: Assign 15 Virupas.10  
* Otherwise: Assign 0 Virupas.

**Output Parameter:** Integer value of ![][image48] Virupas.10

## ---

**DIG BALA: Directional Strength Vector**

Dig Bala evaluates a planet's spatial alignment with its cardinal point of maximum directional emanation. The algorithm necessitates the absolute longitude of the planet against the precise computational cusps of the houses, specifically the Ascendant (1st), Nadir (4th), Descendant (7th), and Meridian/Midheaven (10th).15

**Input Variables:**

* **![][image2]**: True sidereal longitude of the planet in decimal degrees.  
* ![][image49]: Exact sidereal longitudes of the relevant house cusps (Bhava Madhya) calculated via the standard quadrant house system (typically Sripathi or Porphyry in traditional computation).17

**Directional Matrix Configuration:**

| Planet(s) | Point of Maximum Strength (Hmax​) | Point of Minimum Strength (Hmin​) |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Sun, Mars | 10th House Cusp (![][image50], South) | 4th House Cusp (![][image51], North) |
| Moon, Venus | 4th House Cusp (![][image51], North) | 10th House Cusp (![][image50], South) |
| Mercury, Jupiter | 1st House Cusp (![][image52], East) | 7th House Cusp (![][image53], West) |
| Saturn | 7th House Cusp (![][image53], West) | 1st House Cusp (![][image52], East) |

**Mathematical Formula:** To compute the continuous gradient of strength, the algorithm measures the spherical arc length between the planet ![][image2] and its point of *minimum* directional strength ![][image54].15

![][image55]  
The resulting maximum possible distance from the weakest point is ![][image1] (which places it at the strongest point). This distance is scaled by dividing by 3\.15

![][image56]  
**Output Parameter:** Float value ranging from ![][image24] to ![][image25] Virupas.15

## ---

**KALA BALA: Temporal Strength Algorithms**

Kala Bala constitutes the most mathematically rigorous and data-intensive module in the Shadbala engine. It comprises nine independent temporal equations that translate celestial events, geocentric astronomical chronometry, and spherical declination mechanics into quantifiable energy metrics.1 The maximum theoretical output for Kala Bala is 360 Virupas.14

### **1\. Nathonnatha Bala (Diurnal/Nocturnal Strength)**

This continuous function measures strength based on the proximity of the birth event to local apparent noon (for day-strong planets) or local apparent midnight (for night-strong planets).14

**Input Variables:**

* **![][image57]**: Local birth time.  
* ![][image58]: Local apparent sunrise time.  
* ![][image59]: Local apparent sunset time.  
* ![][image60]: Local Apparent Noon.  
* ![][image61]: Local Apparent Midnight.

**Mathematical Formula:**

1. The engine computes the absolute time differential between the birth time ![][image57] and the nearest Midnight ![][image61], converting the result into classical Ghatis (![][image62]). This variable is defined as **Unnata**.11  
2. The counter-variable **Nata** is derived: ![][image63].11  
3. The engine routes the variables through a planetary scalar:  
   * **Moon, Mars, Saturn** (Nocturnal polarity): ![][image64].11  
   * **Sun, Jupiter, Venus** (Diurnal polarity): ![][image65].11  
   * **Mercury** (Crepuscular/Neutral): Escapes the function and automatically receives a static constant of 60.0 Virupas.18

**Output Parameter:** Float value ranging from ![][image24] to ![][image25] Virupas.14

### **2\. Paksha Bala (Lunar Phase Strength)**

Paksha Bala evaluates the elongation angle between the Moon and the Sun to derive the specific lunar phase (Tithi) and assigns algorithmic strength based on whether the planet acts as a functional benefic or malefic in the chart.19

**Input Variables:**

* **![][image66]**: Absolute Moon Longitude.  
* ![][image67]: Absolute Sun Longitude.

**Mathematical Formula:** First, the raw elongation angle ![][image68] is calculated as the forward distance from the Sun to the Moon.21

![][image69]  
Calculate the base phase scalar (![][image70]) by dividing the elongation by 3:

![][image71]  
**Algorithmic Logic:**

1. For **Benefic Planets** (Jupiter, Venus, Mercury, and the Moon if ![][image72] indicating a Waxing phase):  
   ![][image73]  
   19  
2. For **Malefic Planets** (Sun, Mars, Saturn, and the Moon if ![][image74] indicating a Waning phase):  
   ![][image75]  
   19  
3. **The Lunar Doubling Parameter**: Classical mechanics dictate that the Paksha Bala calculated specifically for the Moon must undergo scalar doubling.19 Therefore, the final Moon value is invariably ![][image76].

**Output Parameter:** Float value ranging from ![][image24] to ![][image25] Virupas for standard planets. The Moon outputs a Float value ranging from ![][image24] to ![][image77] Virupas.19

### **3\. Tribhaga Bala (Tripartite Division Strength)**

The duration of local daylight and local nighttime are each trisected into mathematically equal segments. A specific planet acts as the ruler of each segment, gaining full strength if the birth event occurs within its temporal jurisdiction.21

**Mathematical Formula:**

1. Calculate the Day Duration ![][image78].  
2. Calculate the Night Duration ![][image79].  
3. Trisect the durations: ![][image80] and ![][image81].23  
4. Evaluate the birth time ![][image57] against the trisections:  
   * **Day Segment 1** (![][image58] to ![][image82]): Mercury \= 60 Virupas.21  
   * **Day Segment 2**: Sun \= 60 Virupas.21  
   * **Day Segment 3**: Saturn \= 60 Virupas.21  
   * **Night Segment 1** (![][image59] to ![][image83]): Moon \= 60 Virupas.21  
   * **Night Segment 2**: Venus \= 60 Virupas.21  
   * **Night Segment 3**: Mars \= 60 Virupas.21  
5. **Universal Constant**: Jupiter represents overarching temporal stability and is injected with a constant 60.0 Virupas irrespective of the segment.21 All non-ruling planets for the active segment receive 0.0 Virupas.

**Output Parameter:** Binary allocation resulting in ![][image24] or ![][image25] Virupas.21

### **4 \- 7\. Horavaramasaabda Bala (Year, Month, Day, and Hour Lords)**

These four chronological rulers are identified utilizing the *Ahargana*, which represents the continuous accumulation of Julian days elapsed from a designated astrological creation epoch.11 The standard Hindu astronomico-astrological year is defined strictly as 360 days, and the month as 30 days, entirely divorced from modern Gregorian/solar parameters.23

**Input Variables:**

* **![][image84]**: Ahargana (Total integer days elapsed since the epoch to the day of birth).  
* ![][image57]: Local birth time.  
* ![][image58]: Local sunrise time of the birth day.

**Algorithmic Logic for the Four Lords:**

**4\. Abda Lord (Year Lord) \- 15 Virupas:** The algorithm calculates the planetary ruler of the day on which the current 360-day astrological year commenced.11

![][image85]  
To find the weekday index of the first day of the year:

![][image86]  
*(Where 1 \= Sunday, 2 \= Monday \[Moon\], 3 \= Tuesday \[Mars\], 4 \= Wednesday \[Mercury\], 5 \= Thursday \[Jupiter\], 6 \= Friday \[Venus\], 0/7 \= Saturday).* The determined planet receives 15.0 Virupas.11

**5\. Masa Lord (Month Lord) \- 30 Virupas:** The algorithm calculates the planetary ruler of the day on which the current 30-day astrological month commenced.11

![][image87]  
To find the weekday index of the first day of the month:

![][image88]  
The determined planet receives 30.0 Virupas.11

**6\. Vara Lord (Weekday Lord) \- 45 Virupas:** This identifies the ruler of the exact weekday of birth. The Vedic weekday strictly begins at ![][image58] (local sunrise).21 If ![][image89], the previous day's lord is used.21

![][image90]  
The determined planet receives 45.0 Virupas.21

**7\. Hora Lord (Planetary Hour Lord) \- 60 Virupas:** The algorithm divides the day and night durations into 24 distinct hours (12 during daylight, 12 during nighttime). The length of a day-hora is ![][image91], and a night-hora is ![][image92].21 The sequence of horas initiates at sunrise with the Vara Lord (determined above) and descends consecutively through the Chaldean sequence of orbital velocities (Saturn, Jupiter, Mars, Sun, Venus, Mercury, Moon).24 The planet ruling the active hora at ![][image57] receives 60.0 Virupas.21

**Output Parameters:** Fixed constants of 15.0, 30.0, 45.0, and 60.0 Virupas respectively. All non-ruling planets in a given category receive 0.0.21

### **8\. Ayana Bala (Equinoctial / Declination Strength)**

Ayana Bala translates the spherical declination (![][image93]) of a planet relative to the celestial equator into quantifiable strength metrics.25 Maximum declination implies maximum or minimum energetic output depending on the planetary polarity.25

**Input Variables:**

* **![][image2]**: Tropical (Sayana) Longitude of the planet. (Sidereal longitude must be adjusted by adding the Ayanamsa).20  
* ![][image94]: Obliquity of the Ecliptic (Astronomical constant of ![][image95] or ![][image96]).25

**Mathematical Formula:**

First, the engine computes the exact celestial declination ![][image93]:

![][image97]  
The continuous Virupa formula applies a linear scalar derived from the maximum obliquity to normalize the result to a 60-point scale:

![][image98]  
![][image99]  
20

**Addition/Subtraction Overrides (![][image100]):** The equation requires a conditional switch based on the planet and its declination hemisphere.25

* **Sun, Mars, Jupiter, Venus**: Apply "![][image101]" for Northern Declination (![][image102]), and "![][image103]" for Southern Declination (![][image104]).25  
* **Moon, Saturn**: Apply "![][image103]" for Northern Declination, and "![][image101]" for Southern Declination.25  
* **Mercury**: Due to its ecliptic proximity, always apply "![][image101]" using the absolute value ![][image105] regardless of hemisphere.25  
* **Special Luminary Rule**: The final calculated value for the Sun is mathematically doubled.20

**Output Parameter:** Float value ranging from ![][image24] to ![][image25] Virupas (Sun ranges ![][image24] to ![][image77]).25

### **9\. Yuddha Bala (Planetary War Strength)**

A Graha Yuddha (Planetary War) algorithm is triggered when two true planets (strictly excluding the Sun, Moon, and Nodes) are within a tight longitudinal conjunction of ![][image106].8

**Mathematical Formula:**

1. **Identify Combatants:** Determine if any two planets among Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus, and Saturn have an absolute longitudinal distance of ![][image106].8  
2. **Identify Victor:** The planet possessing the lower celestial longitude (or mathematically higher declination/latitude in advanced 3D models) is classified as the victor.8  
3. **Compute Pre-War Strength (PWS):** Calculate the intermediate strength sum for both combatants:  
   ![][image107]  
   *(Crucial Computational Note: Ayana Bala is strictly excluded from this intermediate variable)*.20  
4. **Access Bimba Parimana Matrix:** Fetch the planetary disc circumference constants 20:  
   * Mars \= 9.4  
   * Mercury \= 6.6  
   * Jupiter \= 190.4  
   * Venus \= 16.6  
   * Saturn \= 158.0  
5. **Calculate Mass-Transfer Differential (![][image108]):**  
   ![][image109]  
6. **Apply Differential:** The differential represents energy siphoned from the loser to the victor.20  
   * Victor's Final Kala Bala \= Initial Kala Bala ![][image110]  
   * Loser's Final Kala Bala \= Initial Kala Bala ![][image111]

**Output Parameter:** Float modification to the aggregate Kala Bala.20

## ---

**CHESHTA BALA: Motional Kinematics Vector**

Cheshta Bala quantifies the strength of a planet based on its apparent geocentric orbital velocity and retrograde kinematics. Slower and retrograde planets are evaluated as possessing concentrated kinetic energy due to their prolonged temporal influence on a specific geometric sector.7

### **1\. Standard Cheshta Bala (Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus, Saturn)**

The core algorithm computes the Cheshta Kendra (Motional Arc) by processing the True Longitude, Mean Longitude, and Seeghrocha (Apogee) of the planet.18

**Input Variables:**

* **![][image112]**: True sidereal longitude of the planet.  
* ![][image66]: Mean sidereal longitude of the planet.  
* ![][image113]: Seeghrocha (Apogee) of the planet.27

**Seeghrocha Constants Array:**

| Planet | Seeghrocha Longitude | Decimal Degrees |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Mars | 7s 28°40' | ![][image114] |
| Mercury | 7s 10°34' | ![][image115] |
| Jupiter | 6s 12°11' | ![][image116] |
| Venus | 7s 14°57' | ![][image117] |
| Saturn | 10s 01°24' | ![][image118] |
| \*\*.28 |  |  |

**Mathematical Formula:**

1. Compute the Average Longitude ![][image119]:  
   ![][image120]  
   18  
2. Compute the Cheshta Kendra ![][image121]:  
   ![][image122]  
   18  
3. Evaluate the continuous spherical magnitude:  
   ![][image123]  
   18  
4. Compute final scaling parameter:  
   ![][image124]  
   18

This linear equation serves as an algorithmic proxy for the 8 discrete states of planetary motion defined in BPHS 27.21-23. The states map analogously to the output curve: Vakra (Retrograde, 60 Virupas), Anuvakra (Entering previous sign retrograde, 50), Margi (Direct, 40), Manda (Slow, 30), Mandatara (Stationary/Slowest, 20), Sama (Average, 10), Atichari (Accelerated, 5).7 The exact continuous equation is preferred in modern computational engines as it prevents stepping artifacts across motion boundaries.

### **2\. Luminary Substitutions (Sun and Moon)**

Because the celestial mechanics of the Sun and Moon do not permit true retrograde motion, computational engines must implement an override function to supply their Cheshta Bala.20 The system re-routes existing variables from the Kala Bala calculations:

* **Sun Cheshta Bala** \= The Sun's previously calculated Ayana Bala.20  
* **Moon Cheshta Bala** \= The Moon's previously calculated Paksha Bala.20

**Output Parameter:** Float value ranging from ![][image24] to ![][image25] Virupas.18

## ---

**NAISARGIKA BALA: Natural Luminosity Constants**

Naisargika Bala reflects the inherent, unalterable cosmological luminosity and mass ratio of the Grahas. These constants are non-variable, hardcoded arrays within the engine architecture. They apply universally to all chart instances, independent of the variable parameters of time, space, or dignity.2

**Mathematical Formula:** The strength matrix distributes 60 Virupas in fractional increments of ![][image125] Rupa (or ![][image126] Virupas) descending mathematically from the Sun down to Saturn, based on classical perceived luminosity.31

| Planet | Algorithmic Fraction | Fixed Virupa Scalar |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Sun | ![][image127] | 60.00 |
| Moon | ![][image128] | 51.43 |
| Venus | ![][image129] | 42.86 |
| Jupiter | ![][image130] | 34.29 |
| Mercury | ![][image131] | 25.71 |
| Mars | ![][image132] | 17.14 |
| Saturn | ![][image133] | 8.57 |

**Nodal Exception:** Within the strict computational framework of classical Shadbala outlined by Parashara, the lunar nodes (Rahu and Ketu) are excluded from the Naisargika matrix.2 Derivative engines that force nodal scoring typically assign them the total Shadbala of their respective sign dispositors, but this is outside the scope of base BPHS mechanics.

**Output Parameter:** Static Float constants per planet.31

## ---

**DRIG BALA: Aspectual Interference Patterns**

Drig Bala models the absolute aspectual interference (Drishti) received by a target planet from all other radiating planets. The algorithm processes intersecting orbital geometries, parsing beneficial wave additions and malefic wave subtractions.32

### **1\. Sphuta Drishti (Continuous Aspect Vector) Formula**

Let ![][image20] represent the exact angular distance in degrees from the radiating (Aspecting) planet to the receiving (Aspected) planet, measured strictly forward through the zodiac.34

![][image134]  
The base aspect value ![][image135] is processed through a complex piecewise function that mirrors the discrete "quarter slabs" (![][image136]) mentioned in classical texts, but translates them into a continuous trigonometric proxy 33:

* If ![][image137]: ![][image138]  
* If ![][image139]: ![][image140]  
* If ![][image141]: ![][image142]  
* If ![][image143]: ![][image144]  
* If ![][image145]: ![][image146]  
* If ![][image147]: ![][image148] (Reaching exactly 60 Virupas at ![][image1], or full aspect)  
* If ![][image149]: ![][image150]  
* If ![][image151]: ![][image150]  
* If ![][image152]: ![][image150]  
* If ![][image153]: ![][image150]  
* If ![][image154]: ![][image138]

### **2\. Vishesha Drishti (Special Aspect Overrides)**

Certain outer planets possess specialized orbital harmonics that override or augment the standard equations at specific geometrical intervals 33:

**Mars Overrides:**

* If ![][image143] (4th House): ![][image155] 33  
* If ![][image145]: ![][image148] 33  
* If ![][image151] (8th House): ![][image156] 33

**Jupiter Overrides:**

* If ![][image145] (5th House): ![][image148] 33  
* If ![][image152] (9th House): ![][image157] 33

**Saturn Overrides:**

* If ![][image139] (3rd House): ![][image158] 33  
* If ![][image141]: ![][image159] 33  
* If ![][image160] (10th House): ![][image161] 33

### **3\. Drig Bala Integration**

Once all aspect vectors ![][image135] onto a target planet are computed, they are bifurcated by the nature of the radiating planet 32:

1. **Benefic Sum (![][image162])**: Sum of ![][image135] from Jupiter, Venus, well-associated Mercury, and the Waxing Moon.32  
2. **Malefic Sum (![][image163])**: Sum of ![][image135] from the Sun, Mars, Saturn, and the Waning Moon.32

**Mathematical Formula:** The ultimate Drig Bala vector compresses the net aspectual interference by scaling it to a quarter factor (Drishti Pinda reduction).18

![][image164]  
**Output Parameter:** Float value ranging from ![][image165] to ![][image166] Virupas (Drig Bala is the only strength capable of producing a negative modifier).32

## ---

**FINAL AGGREGATION AND MINIMUM THRESHOLDS**

The final computational quotient for each planet is the unweighted scalar sum of the six matrices:

![][image167]  
1

To programmatically flag a planet's operational state as "Strong" (capable of delivering optimal results) or "Weak", the engine evaluates the resulting scalar against the exact threshold minimums explicitly defined in BPHS 27.32-33.1 Because the output is required in Rupas, the scalar sum must be divided by 60\.1

**BPHS 27.32-33 Validation Array:**

| Planet | Required Minimum (Virupas) | Required Minimum (Rupas) |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Mercury | 420.0 | 7.0 |
| Jupiter | 390.0 | 6.5 |
| Sun | 390.0 | 6.5 |
| Moon | 360.0 | 6.0 |
| Venus | 330.0 | 5.5 |
| Saturn | 300.0 | 5.0 |
| Mars | 300.0 | 5.0 |

**System Logic:** IF (Shadbala\_{total} / 60\) \>= Required\_Rupas THEN State \= STRONG ELSE State \= WEAK.3

## ---

**BHAVA BALA: House Strength Computation**

To accurately predict the functional viability of specific domains of life, the software engine must execute a secondary analytical pass over the 12 astrological houses. The total Bhava Bala evaluates the structural capacity of the house itself and is comprised of three matrices: Bhavadhipati Bala, Bhava Dig Bala, and Bhava Drishti Bala.38

### **1\. Bhavadhipati Bala (Lord's Strength Contribution)**

This parameter is an exact inheritance of the Shadbala strength of the planet that naturally rules the zodiac sign falling on the specific Bhava Madhya (House Cusp).6

**Mathematical Formula:**

**![][image168]**  
For example, if the 4th house cusp mathematically intersects Aries, Mars is the Lord, and Mars's total Shadbala Virupas are directly imported as the Bhavadhipati Bala for the 4th house.38

### **2\. Bhava Dig Bala (House Directional Strength)**

Bhava Dig Bala operates mechanically similar to planetary Dig Bala, utilizing distance interpolation. However, it relies on the biological taxonomy (Tattvas) of the zodiac signs falling on the house cusps. Each house is scored based on the distance of its cusp from the peak directional power of the biological group governing its sign.40

**Sign Taxonomy Matrix:**

* **Nara (Human / Biped)**: Gemini, Virgo, Libra, Aquarius, and the 1st half of Sagittarius (![][image169]).42  
* **Jalachara (Aquatic)**: Cancer, Pisces, and the 2nd half of Capricorn (![][image170]).40  
* **Chatushpada (Quadruped)**: Aries, Taurus, Leo, the 2nd half of Sagittarius (![][image170]), and the 1st half of Capricorn (![][image169]).40  
* **Keeta (Insect)**: Scorpio.40

**House Power Constraints (Peak 60.0 Virupas, Minimum 0.0 Virupas):**

* **Nara** signs attain maximum 60 Virupas at the 1st House (Ascendant, ![][image52]) and 0 at the 7th House (![][image53]).40  
* **Jalachara** signs attain maximum 60 Virupas at the 4th House (Nadir, ![][image51]) and 0 at the 10th House (![][image50]).40  
* **Chatushpada** signs attain maximum 60 Virupas at the 10th House (Meridian, ![][image50]) and 0 at the 4th House (![][image51]).40  
* **Keeta** signs attain maximum 60 Virupas at the 7th House (Descendant, ![][image53]) and 0 at the 1st House (![][image52]).40

**Mathematical Formula:** Let ![][image171] denote the exact longitude of the house cusp in question, and ![][image172] denote the longitude of the house cusp where the sign's biological category holds 0 strength.41

![][image173]  
![][image174]  
**Output Parameter:** Float value ranging from ![][image24] to ![][image25] Virupas per house.41

### **3\. Bhava Drishti Bala (Aspectual House Strength)**

This calculates the aggregate aspectual radiation cast by all planets directly onto the exact longitude of the Bhava Madhya (House Cusp). The geometry mirrors planetary Drig Bala, treating the house cusp as the receiving entity.38

**Mathematical Formula:**

1. Substitute the ![][image175] value in the **Sphuta Drishti** piecewise equations (detailed in the Drig Bala section) with the exact longitude of the Bhava Madhya.33  
2. Calculate the base continuous aspect scalar ![][image135] for every planet interacting with the house cusp.  
3. Compute the Malefic and Benefic sums (![][image163] and ![][image162]).  
4. Calculate the net difference and apply the quartering scalar 41:  
   ![][image176]

*Note for computational edge cases: While standard planetary Drig Bala can yield negative results, negative Bhava Drishti values subtract from the total structural integrity of the house, representing systemic damage to the areas of life governed by that house.*

### **Final Bhava Bala Integration**

![][image177]  
38

The resulting absolute variables evaluate the structural capacity of a specific life domain to manifest results autonomously, functioning as the foundational baseline against which the operational capacities of the planets inhabiting it are ultimately measured.5

#### **Works cited**

1. Shadbala Calculator \- Planetary Strength in Virupas \- Muhuratam, accessed on March 2, 2026, [https://muhuratam.in/shadbala-calculator](https://muhuratam.in/shadbala-calculator)  
2. Shadbala An Overview | PDF | Equator | Planets \- Scribd, accessed on March 2, 2026, [https://ru.scribd.com/doc/169114711/Shadbala-an-Overview](https://ru.scribd.com/doc/169114711/Shadbala-an-Overview)
